title: Nami Learns About the Log Pose
description: On pages 5–7, nami learns about the log pose as luffy turns Laboon’s grief into a promise of a future rematch, and the crew receives the Log Pose needed for the Grand Line.
scene: Nami Learns About the Log Pose. Luffy turns Laboon’s grief into a promise of a future rematch, and the crew receives the Log Pose needed for the Grand Line. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I... had no idea.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: What!? Isn't that like really really bad? Are we gonna be OK!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Ah, Nami's so cute even when she doesn't know!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Huh? Just what about this fish is bad?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: You two just quiet down!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: This elephant trout trunk is especially delicious!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: To navigate through Grand Line, you'll need a log pose.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: A log pose?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I've never heard of it.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: It's a special type of compass that records the magnetic field.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: So it's a weird- looking compass?

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: Well, it is rather oddly shaped as well.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: You mean something like this?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Yes, that's it.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: without a log pose, it'd be impossible to navigate on this ocean.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Of course, it is quite hard to obtain one outside Grand Line.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: I see, but hang on just a second...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Why the heck do you have one!?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Those two weirdos from earlier,

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: them?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: accidentally left it on our ship.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: Why'd you hit me?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Just felt I had to.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Oh.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: So this is a log pose...

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: There's no marking on it or anything...

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: The various islands across Grand Line,

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: All have their own magnetic fields surrounding them.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: So in short, you'll have to record the magnetic field interaction between any two islands,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Before you can progress on to the next island.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: In this ocean where one can't use any normal means of path-finding,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: From this mountain, you'll choose 1 out of 7 magnetic fields to record, which'll lead you to different islands.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: But no matter which island you start with,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: The paths will all coverage into one eventually...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: And the name of the very last island one can reach in Grand Line is...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Rafiel,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: The final stop in Grand Line.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: The only ones who've ever confirmed its existence in history is the pirate King and his crew.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: It is an island of legends.

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on nami learns about the log pose. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
